system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;

maxCo           1;

maxDeltaT       1;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (5 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //
'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Back face (z = -0.005)
    ( -0.15242  0        -0.005 )   // 0
    (  0.3048   0        -0.005 )   // 1
    (  0.3048   0.1524   -0.005 )   // 2
    ( -0.15242  0.1524   -0.005 )   // 3

    // Front face (z = 0.005)
    ( -0.15242  0         0.005 )   // 4
    (  0.3048   0         0.005 )   // 5
    (  0.3048   0.1524    0.005 )   // 6
    ( -0.15242  0.1524    0.005 )   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (100 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //
')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: `log.blockMesh` — Face Already Belongs to Some Other Patch (Persistent Issue)

**Error Message:**
```
Trying to specify a boundary face 4(0 1 2 3) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch. 
This is face 0 of patch 2 named frontAndBack.
```

**Root Cause — The Real Problem Across All Attempts:**

The fundamental issue is that the **face `(0 1 2 3)`** (the back face at z = -0.005) is being assigned to the **frontAndBack** patch, but it is ALSO being classified as an **internal face**. This happens because the vertex ordering of the hex block creates a situation where this face is not a true external boundary.

Looking at the vertex coordinates carefully:
- Vertex 0: (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- Vertex 1: (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
- Vertex 2: (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
- Vertex 3: (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)

These all have z = -0.005. Now look at the **second quad** (vertices 4-7):
- Vertex 4: (-0.15242, 0, 0.005)
- Vertex 5: (0.3048, 0, 0.005)
- Vertex 6: (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)
- Vertex 7: (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005)

All have z = +0.005.

The hex block `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` stacks these two quads. The face `(0 1 2 3)` is the bottom quad — but crucially, **this face is coplanar with nothing else in the mesh**. It SHOULD be a valid external face. The fact that it's flagged as internal means the block topology interpretation considers it internal.

**The breakthrough realization:** In OpenFOAM's `blockMesh`, when you have a hex block where one quad is at z=-0.005 and the other at z=+0.005, the face formed by vertices 0,1,2,3 is indeed the "bottom" face. But the error saying "face 4(0 1 2 3)" means this face is being treated as an **internal face between block 0 and... nothing** — which suggests the block topology is malformed.

**The actual fix:** The vertex ordering of the hex block needs to be corrected so that the face `(0 1 2 3)` is recognized as a proper external face. Try reversing the vertex ordering of the entire hex block:

Change:
```
hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (100 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

To:
```
hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4) (100 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

This reverses the winding so that the face that was previously `(0 1 2 3)` now has its normal pointing in the opposite direction, potentially resolving the "internal face" detection.

**Alternatively, and more robustly:** Split the domain into **two blocks** to avoid the single-hex topology ambiguity entirely. Define two stacked blocks sharing an interface:

- **Block A**: vertices (0, 1, 5, 4, 3, 2, 6, 7) — essentially the full domain split diagonally
- Actually, define two rectangular blocks side-by-side along the wedge:

Define 5 vertices to create two adjacent blocks:
Vertices:
0: (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
1: (0, 0, -0.005) ← NEW vertex at the wedge apex at origin, z=-0.005
2: (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
3: (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)
4: (0, 0.1524, -0.005)
5: (-0.15242, 0, 0.005)
6: (0, 0, 0.005)
7: (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
8: (0, 0.1524, 0.005)
9: (0.3048, 0, 0.005)

Then define two blocks:
- Block 1: hex (0 1 4 3 5 6 9 8) — lower-left region
- Block 2: hex (1 2 7 4 6 9 10 11) — lower-right region

But this is complex. **Simpler approach:** Reduce to just **three boundary patches** as suggested in Attempt 4, but ensure the face `(0 1 2 3)` is NOT assigned to frontAndBack. Instead, assign ONLY the two z-faces `(0 1 2 3)` and `(4 5 6 7)` to frontAndBack, and assign the face `(0 1 2 3)` to NO PATCH — remove it from the boundary section entirely. Wait, that face CANNOT be removed — it must go somewhere.

**Definitive fix:** The face `(0 1 2 3)` is the **actual bottom face** of the hex (z=-0.005 plane). This face is NOT the wedge face — the wedge face is `(0 3 2 1)` (same vertices, reversed order, representing the y=0 plane at the bottom). The face `(0 1 2 3)` at z=-0.005 is a legitimate external face and should be included in **frontAndBack** along with `(4 5 6 7)`. The error means this face is considered internal. This can only happen if the block topology treats it as such due to winding/ordering.

Try swapping the entire vertex ordering of the hex to:
```
hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (100 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

This rotates the block so that the face originally at `(0 1 2 3)` becomes a side face with a different normal orientation.

## Error: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cascading

This error will resolve once `blockMesh` succeeds.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change hex block vertex ordering from (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) to (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) so that the face (0 1 2 3) is no longer interpreted as an internal face; this reorders the block to rotate the geometry and prevent the face conflict in frontAndBack'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
